notes: FOREIGN (liquidation-bot tx; own history has no Suilend liquidation — this is the U0-spike sample tx from src/types/suilend-events.ts). Decoded from the LIQUIDATOR's perspective (walletsContext = tx sender): Suilend's LiquidateEvent carries only obligation_id, never the obligation owner's address, so a stateless handler CANNOT attribute the liquidated-user side (and a liquidation of an own obligation is never ingested anyway — the bot is sender and recipient; needs the Phase-1D obligation_id->wallet map, see handler header). LiquidateEvent idx 11: repay 2360703 Wormhole SOL (8 dec), withdraw_amount 50548385545 WAL CTOKENS, protocol_fee 1419285124 ctokens; liquidator nets 50548385545 - 1419285124 = 49129100421 ctokens, redeemed by the paired RedeemEvent idx 13 (49129100421 ctokens -> 52552401677 WAL underlying, liquidator_bonus_amount 1661370546 ctokens included). Labeled: liquidation:collateral_seized (seq 0, received 52552401677 WAL — the paired Redeem folds into the seizure, NO separate lend_supply:withdraw) + liquidation:debt_repaid (seq 1, sent 2360703 SOL), both at the LiquidateEvent index (mirrors navi-02). Bot wraps everything in a Bluefin flash loan (pkg 0x1e4a13a0..., idx 0/1/16/17) + Cetus swaps (idx 2/14) — not Suilend's events, no TaxEvent from this handler.
